Fetch FWA Stream Network Segments

Source: R/frs_stream_fetch.R
frs_stream_fetch.Rd

Retrieve stream segments from a stream network table. Filter by watershed group code, blue line key, and/or bounding box.

Usage

frs_stream_fetch(
  conn,
  watershed_group_code = NULL,
  blue_line_key = NULL,
  bbox = NULL,
  stream_order_min = NULL,
  table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp",
  cols = c("linear_feature_id", "blue_line_key", "waterbody_key", "edge_type",
    "gnis_name", "stream_order", "stream_magnitude", "gradient",
    "downstream_route_measure", "upstream_route_measure", "length_metre",
    "watershed_group_code", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree", "geom"),
  include_all = FALSE,
  limit = NULL
)

Arguments

conn

A DBI::DBIConnection object (from frs_db_conn()).

watershed_group_code

Character. Watershed group code (e.g. "BULK"). Default NULL.

blue_line_key

Integer. Blue line key for a specific stream. Default NULL.

bbox

Numeric vector of length 4 (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) in BC Albers (EPSG:3005). Default NULL.

stream_order_min

Integer. Minimum Strahler stream order to return. Default NULL (all orders).

table

Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp".

cols

Character vector of column names to select. Default includes the most commonly used FWA stream attributes.

include_all

Logical. If TRUE, include placeholder streams (999 wscode) and unmapped tributaries (NULL localcode). Default FALSE filters these out. Only applied when querying the FWA base table.

limit

Integer. Maximum rows to return. Default NULL (no limit).

Value

An sf data frame of stream segments.

See also

Other fetch: frs_lake_fetch(), frs_wetland_fetch()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
conn <- frs_db_conn()

# All streams in the Bulkley watershed group
streams <- frs_stream_fetch(conn, watershed_group_code = "BULK")

# Streams with order >= 4
streams <- frs_stream_fetch(conn, watershed_group_code = "BULK",
  stream_order_min = 4)
DBI::dbDisconnect(conn)
} # }
